header.title: Security hardening
header.subtitle: Audit based on <0>Lynis</0> (open-source, read-only). No change is applied — fixes will be offered in one click.
header.empty: No audit yet. Run an audit to assess this machine's security posture.

stale.text: Last known posture{{auditDate}}. The detail of warnings/suggestions is not kept — re-run the audit to see it.

watchdog.hintFirewall: Check you still have access to the machine.
watchdog.revert: Without confirmation, the previous state will be restored automatically.

remediations.title: Recommended remediations
remediations.sshNote: SSH hardening validates the config (`sshd -t`) then reloads via SIGHUP with a 120s watchdog (anti-lock-out).
remediations.postureDirty: Measures applied. Re-run an audit to recompute the index and findings (you can chain other fixes first).
remediations.disabledTitle: Restricted to AGENT machines / authorized role
remediations.sysctlNetworkLabel: Harden network/kernel sysctl (ICMP redirects, source routing, syncookies…)

firewallWizard.title: Firewall wizard
firewallWizard.intro: Detects listening services (non-loopback) and proposes a policy: allow the checked ports + block all inbound by default.
firewallWizard.emptyAll: No exposed service detected (all on loopback).
firewallWizard.emptyNonDocker: No non-Docker exposed service. All exposed ports are published by Docker (managed by its own iptables rules) — nothing to add in ufw.
firewallWizard.applyHint: Enable ufw (deny inbound) + allow the checked ports. 60s anti-lock-out watchdog.
firewallWizard.dockerPorts_one: {{count}} port managed by Docker — excluded from the ufw policy
firewallWizard.dockerPorts_other: {{count}} ports managed by Docker — excluded from the ufw policy
firewallWizard.dockerHint: Docker inserts its own iptables rules (DOCKER chain); ufw doesn't filter them. To restrict these ports, publish them on a specific IP (<0>-p 127.0.0.1:…</0>) or via an upstream firewall.

confirmFirewall.title: Apply this firewall policy?
confirmFirewall.desc: Enables ufw (deny inbound by default) and allows: {{ports}}. 60s watchdog: if you lose access or don't confirm, the previous state is restored automatically.
confirmFirewall.sshWarning: ⚠️ WARNING: no SSH port detected/selected — risk of locking yourself out.

lynisFooter: Lynis {{version}} · the hardening score is an indicator of measures taken, not a percentage of "security".

bannerDialog.defaultBanner:
*** Restricted access ***
Unauthorized access to this system is prohibited and may be subject to
prosecution. All activity may be logged and monitored.
bannerDialog.desc: Warning shown BEFORE login (console + SSH). Customizable.
bannerDialog.hint: No effect on access. Max 4096 characters.

f2bDialog.desc: Installs fail2ban if absent, deploys an SSH jail and enables the service.
f2bDialog.durationsHint: Durations: seconds or shorthand (<0>600</0>, <1>10m</1>, <2>1h</2>, <3>1d</3>, <4>1w</4>). SSH is protected by default.

preview.exactContent: Exact content that will be applied (read from the agent, not a copy):

toasts.sshApplied: SSH hardening applied — confirm within 120s (test your reconnection).
toasts.policyApplied: Policy applied — confirm within 60s (check your access).

auditDialog.title: Hardening audit
auditDialog.timeout: Timed out: no response from the agent. Check the agent / the Lynis sudoers.
auditDialog.analyzing: Lynis is analyzing the configuration (firewall, SSH, kernel, accounts, updates…)
auditDialog.successText: Audit done — hardening index <0>{{score}}</0>. Detail in the tab.
